title: Prose Audit Portable Map
description: A portable, project-neutral structured map. It is a DERIVED index of an authoritative map/canon (e.g. the-war-for-paratua canon/pages/geography/map_of_paratua.md and the campaign map image); it never replaces that source of truth. It supports qualitative relationships (bearings, adjacency) where coordinates are unknown, and marks uncertainty explicitly rather than forcing false precision. See PROSE_AUDIT_CAPABILITY_SPEC.md section E.

provenance.description: Where this derived index came from and how current it is. This index is not canon; it points at canon.

derived_from.description: Authoritative sources this index was built from (paths/names only).

captured_at.description: ISO date the index was captured/reviewed. Passed in; never machine-clock-derived here.

bearing_vocabulary.description: The permitted qualitative bearings used by places[].relative_bearings and routes[].bearing.

place_id.description: Stable canonical id, kebab-case. e.g. luthan-rise, luthan-mts, cirshe, putakta.

canonical_name.description: The name canon uses in prose.

aliases.description: Other names/spellings that refer to this same place. Used to resolve prose mentions. An alias is a name, not a separate place.

confusable_with.description: Place ids this place is commonly conflated with (e.g. luthan-rise vs luthan-mts). Drives high-value consistency checks.

terrain.description: Terrain descriptors. A prose terrain word that contradicts these (e.g. calling hills 'mountains') is a candidate contradiction.

contained_by.description: place_id of the region/containment parent, or null. Supports adjacency/containment checks.

coordinates.description: Grid coordinates when known from the map sheet (0-80 on both axes). Null when unknown. Qualitative relationships must still be usable without this.

relative_bearings.description: Qualitative bearing of this place relative to a reference place, used when coordinates are unknown. e.g. luthan-rise is S of cirshe.

adjacency.description: place_ids that physically neighbor this place (share a border/route endpoint).

source_ref.description: Authoritative source for this place record.

routes.description: Named or endpoint-defined routes between places, with mode and any required crossings/barriers.

name.description: Canon road name if any, e.g. great-western-road.

sea_character.description: For sea/mixed routes: 'coastal' (following a shoreline) vs 'open-crossing' (across open water). Drives the Cirshe->Putakta 'by sea south' vs 'across the ocean' check.

bearing.description: Overall direction of travel from from_place to to_place.

required_crossings.description: Barriers that must be crossed, e.g. a river ford, a pass. Used to catch routes that ignore a required crossing.

barriers_avoided.description: Barriers the canonical route deliberately avoids (e.g. a closed pass, a river crossing).
